# DOSSIER: Jon Benson

> **Versão:** 3.0.0
> **Criado:** 2026-03-22
> **Atualizado:** 2026-03-23
> **Tipo:** PERSON (Expert)
> **Status:** COMPLETE (7 sources — 3XVSL + CVSL + 100M + 28DAY + FT + TP + TCB)

---

## IDENTIDADE

**Jon Benson** — Criador do formato Video Sales Letter (VSL). Pioneiro do marketing direto em vídeo com texto-em-tela. Desenvolveu o sistema 3X VSL Formula que se tornou o padrão da indústria para vendas online via vídeo. Mais recentemente, pioneiro em AI Copywriting com o sistema Chat VSL — usando ChatGPT para criar VSLs completas via prompts e pre-prompting.

**Background:**
- Copywriter de direct response (inventor do formato VSL)
- Criador do "ugly VSL" genre (text-on-screen format)
- Autor do Every Other Day Diet (primeiro produto VSL em escala)
- Criador do Metabolic Sherpa VSL ($50M em receita, rodando desde 2015)
- Fundador do Copy Collective ($769/mês mastermind)
- Revenue proof: $4B+ para clientes usando sua fórmula
- Pioneiro em AI copywriting (ChatVSL, CopyPro, Vincent/Benson AI)

## CONTRIBUIÇÃO ÚNICA

Jon Benson transformou o copywriting de resposta direta em DUAS ondas:

### Onda 1: Invenção do VSL (3X VSL Formula)
1. **Formato VSL** — Texto em tela com narração (vs. talking head ou carta escrita)
2. **Psicologia de Continuação** — Action drops, anchor drops, open loops (10-12 por VSL)
3. **Pattern Interrupt System** — Movie-scene format, anchor objects, brain scratching
4. **Conspiracy Cycle** — Reframing do problema como algo maior que o indivíduo
5. **USP-Driven Architecture** — Todo o VSL se constrói ao redor de uma única proposição

### Onda 2: AI Copywriting (Chat VSL)
6. **Buyer's Roadmap Pre-Prompting** — 12 campos de dados ANTES de qualquer prompt
7. **12-Prompt Library** — Sistema reutilizável de prompts para cada parte da VSL
8. **Two-Shot In-Context Training** — Exemplos > instruções para AI (nightmare stories)
9. **Dimensionalization** — Métrica de qualidade: "Can you SEE it? Can you FEEL it?"
10. **9 Attention Frames** — N1, N2, N3, O1, O2, W3 + 90 Sec Desire + Future Pacing + NLP Close

### Onda 3: Copywriting de Persuasão Profunda (The Copywriter Blueprint)
11. **The Hex** — 6-sided persuasion wheel cobrindo avatars, complaints, beliefs, goals, solutions, actions
12. **Persuasion Quadrant (WNFA)** — Wants, Needs, Fears, Actions como matriz de copy
13. **Empathy Formula** — See-Hear-Validate como protocolo de conexão emocional
14. **Love Letter Marketing** — Service, Empathy, Hope como estrutura de mensagem
15. **Word Alchemy** — Transformação de palavras técnicas em linguagem emocional e vivida
16. **Self-Identifying Avatars (3 Tiers)** — Segmentação tridimensional de persona para copy hiper-relevante
17. **Speedwriting 10-Step Method** — Método de escrita rápida que preserva voz e intenção
18. **Samurai PS/Q&A** — Técnica de pós-script + Q&A que adiciona +30% de conversão
19. **Tsunami 4-Part Close** — Crossroads → Future → Rights → Smarts (sequência de fechamento)
20. **Triple Guarantee** — Estrutura de garantia tripla para eliminar risco percebido
21. **USP 5-Component Formula** — Formulação completa da proposta única de valor
22. **Sales Bullet 5-Part Formula** — Estrutura de bala de vendas de alta conversão
23. **Chiasmus 5-Point Formula** — Técnica retórica de inversão para memorabilidade e impacto

---

## FILOSOFIA CENTRAL

> "If you do not get your USP right, everything crumbles."
> "AI is a better rewriter than it is a writer."
> "Pre-prompts are so much cooler than prompts."

Jon Benson opera sob a premissa de que vender é sobre **transformar o estado mental do prospect de DECISÃO para DESEJO**. Na era AI, ele adiciona que **a qualidade do output depende 80% dos dados de input (pre-prompting)**.

- **Rapport antes de pitch** — Nightmare story cria conexão emocional
- **Expert from necessity** — Posicionar-se como "um pouco melhor" que o prospect
- **Emotional selling** — 85% compram por emoção; a lógica vem depois no Grand Offer
- **AI como amplificador** — Hands on the steering wheel (Tesla analogy)
- **Sell what they want** — USP vem do comprador, não do vendedor
- **Dimensionalize everything** — Copy que não cria imagem mental está morta
- **Soft selling is unethical** — Se você acredita no produto, faça o caso mais forte
- **80% after first pitch** — Continue vendendo muito além do primeiro CTA

---

## FRAMEWORKS SIGNATURE

### Buyer's Roadmap (3 Parts) — CVSL
```
Part 1: SEARCH 1 (12 data fields: niche, avatar, goals, complaints, promises,
         benefits, objections, fears, false solutions, mistaken beliefs, alternatives)
Part 2: UNIQUE MECHANISMS (negative hook + positive hook, 3 words each)
Part 3: OFFER BRANDING (name, price, guarantee, bonuses, testimonials)
```

### ChatVSL 5-Step Formula — CVSL
```
Step 1: SNAP SUGGESTION (~1000w — pattern interrupt as movie scene + lead)
Step 2: VITAL CONNECTION (~1500w — nightmare story + Declaration of Independence)
Step 3: BIG PROBLEM (~500w — negative hook reveal + Big Lie)
Step 4: BIGGER SOLUTION (~800w — positive hook + 2-3 tips)
Step 5: GRAND OFFER (~2200w — 6-part perfect pitch)
Total: ~6000 words / ~40 minutes
```

### Grand Offer 6-Part Structure — CVSL
```
1. 3 Choices Intro (do nothing / DIY / smart path)
2. Product Features (So Rather Than X, You Get To Y)
3. Faux Pricing (retail >2x offer = 80%+ perceived discount)
4. Dimensional Bonuses (That Means You Get To...)
5. Guarantee + Community Inclusion
6. NLP Close (Future Pacing 7d+30d + Presupposition + Click Anchor + CTA)
```

### 9 Attention Frames — CVSL
```
N1: Notice the VSL (first 7 seconds — multiple pattern interrupts)
N2: Notice the hero (modest introduction)
N3: Permission to be noticed (psychological permission for transformation)
O1: Negative hook reveal (root cause)
O2: Positive hook reveal (secret solution)
W3: Killer wants ("works even if" objection handling)
+: 90 Seconds of Desire (post-interrupt benefit stack)
+: Future Pacing (7 days + 30 days, present tense)
+: NLP Close (presupposition + click anchor)
```

### 1-2-3 Formula — 3XVSL
```
1 USP + 2 Stories + 3 Tips + 4 Comparisons
```

### Conspiracy Cycle — 3XVSL
```
Big Lie → Not Your Fault → Blame This → Big Truth → Open Bigger Solution Loop
```

### 12-Prompt Library — CVSL
```
Buyer's Roadmap | Refiner | Branding | Expansion | Rewrite Sections
Offer Branding | Pattern Interrupt (x2) | Lead | Different Goals
Get Specific | Bullet Expander | Create Returns/Slides
```

### The Hex (6-Sided Persuasion Framework) — TCB
```
Side 1: AVATARS      → Who are they? (3-tier self-identifying segmentation)
Side 2: COMPLAINTS   → What do they say? (language mining for copy)
Side 3: BELIEFS      → What do they believe? (limiting vs. empowering)
Side 4: GOALS        → What do they want? (stated + unstated desires)
Side 5: SOLUTIONS    → What have they tried? (pre-frame existing failures)
Side 6: ACTIONS      → What will motivate them to act? (triggers + barriers)
```

### Persuasion Quadrant (WNFA) — TCB
```
W: WANTS  → What they consciously desire (stated)
N: NEEDS  → What they actually require (gap between want and need)
F: FEARS  → What stops them from buying (internal + external objections)
A: ACTIONS → What specific behaviors you're asking them to take
```

### Empathy Formula — TCB
```
SEE   → Acknowledge what they see (mirror their observable situation)
HEAR  → Validate what they hear (echo their internal dialogue)
VALIDATE → Confirm their feelings are legitimate ("of course you feel that way")
```

### Love Letter Marketing — TCB
```
SERVICE → Lead with how you serve them (not what you sell)
EMPATHY → Show you understand their struggle deeply
HOPE    → Offer a believable path to their desired outcome
```

### Tsunami 4-Part Close — TCB
```
1. CROSSROADS  → Present the fork in the road (status quo vs. change)
2. FUTURE      → Vivid future-pacing of transformed life
3. RIGHTS      → Remind them they deserve this outcome
4. SMARTS      → The logical case — prove this is the intelligent choice
```

### USP 5-Component Formula — TCB
```
1. WHO       → Specific avatar (not "everyone")
2. WHAT      → Specific outcome (not features)
3. HOW       → Unique mechanism (not "better")
4. WHEN      → Specific timeframe (believable)
5. WITHOUT   → Eliminating the #1 objection/fear
```

### Sales Bullet 5-Part Formula — TCB
```
1. HOOK      → Curiosity trigger (the "what")
2. BENEFIT   → Specific positive outcome
3. MECHANISM → How it works (the "why")
4. PROOF     → Evidence or credibility element
5. CTA       → Micro-action or emotional pull
```

### Chiasmus 5-Point Formula — TCB
```
Chiasmus = rhetorical inversion for memorability
Pattern: A-B → B-A (reversed structure creates impact)
5 Points of application: Headlines, Guarantees, Benefits, Objections, CTAs
Example: "We don't change copy to fit the market — we find the market that fits the copy"
```

### Speedwriting 10-Step Method — TCB
```
Step 1:  DUMP    → Brain dump without editing
Step 2:  ANCHOR  → Identify emotional anchor point
Step 3:  VOICE   → Set voice before writing (not after)
Step 4:  AVATAR  → Write to ONE specific person
Step 5:  PROBLEM → Lead with their problem (not your solution)
Step 6:  PROOF   → Insert social proof early
Step 7:  BRIDGE  → Transition from problem to solution
Step 8:  STACK   → Layer benefits dimensionally
Step 9:  CLOSE   → Use Tsunami 4-Part Close
Step 10: POLISH  → Word Alchemy (technical → emotional)
```
